meta.description: BTS News Bank — catalogue of UK energy/gas/plumbing news stories for insights posts

stories.title: InstallerSHOW 2026 — UK's Largest Heating and Plumbing Trade Show Returns to NEC
stories.summary: InstallerSHOW 2026 runs 23-25 June at NEC Birmingham. 40,000+ visitors, 900+ exhibitors, 11 CPD-accredited content streams. Co-located with World Plumbing Conference (CIPHE/World Plumbing Council). Covers HVAC, plumbing, renewables, electrical, and built environment. Free registration. Viessmann launching new residential ASHP at the show.
stories.blogAngle: InstallerSHOW 2026 Is Next Month — What the UK's Biggest Heating Trade Show Tells Us About the Industry
stories.notes: NEW 2026-05-25. TIME-SENSITIVE — event is 23-25 June. Draft insights post before event if using. Scale of event (40k visitors, 900 exhibitors) shows industry health. World Plumbing Conference co-location adds authority.

stories.title: Boiler Upgrade Scheme Confirmed Extended to 2030 — Long-Term Market Signal for Heat Pump Installers
stories.summary: BUS confirmed extended to 2030 as of 28 April 2026. £7,500 grants for air-to-water and ground-source heat pumps. New £2,500 for air-to-air. Scheme extension gives long-term certainty for installers investing in heat pump training and MCS certification. £295M budget for 2025/26.
stories.blogAngle: The BUS Is Here Until 2030 — What Five More Years of Heat Pump Grants Means for Installers
stories.notes: NEW 2026-05-25. Complements stories #37, #38, #39. Extension to 2030 is the key signal — gives installers confidence to invest in training. Angle: long-term market, not short-term grant grab.

stories.title: BUS Grant Rises to £9,000 for Off-Grid Oil and LPG Homes — July 2026
stories.summary: Government announced temporary BUS uplift from £7,500 to £9,000 for off-gas-grid homes replacing oil or LPG heating with air-to-water or ground-source heat pumps. Extra £1,500 top-up expected from July 2026 through March 2027. Only for properties not connected to gas grid. Biomass and air-to-air excluded from uplift.
stories.blogAngle: Heat Pump Grants Just Went Up to £9,000 for Off-Grid Homes — What Installers Need to Know
stories.notes: NEW 2026-05-25. SIGNIFICANT UPDATE. Directly connects heat pump AND LPG training — off-grid LPG homes are prime candidates for heat pump conversion. Installer demand angle: bigger grants = more homeowner uptake = need more qualified installers. Same grant caution applies (installer angle, not homeowner eligibility).

stories.title: Trade Courses ROI — Which Qualifications Pay Back Fastest in 2026
stories.summary: Analysis of which UK trade qualifications deliver the fastest return on investment in 2026. Gas, plumbing, and heat pump certifications highlighted as high-ROI due to skills shortage and rising wages. Useful context for career changers weighing up training costs.
stories.blogAngle: Which Trade Qualifications Pay Back Fastest? A 2026 ROI Breakdown
stories.notes: NEW 2026-05-20. Source is a competitor (YTA Training) — our angle must be unique. Strong career-changer hook.

stories.title: Engineering Salary Survey 2026 — Pay Rising Across All Disciplines
stories.summary: Annual engineering salary survey shows pay rising across all disciplines in 2026. Skills shortages driving competition for qualified engineers. HVAC and building services engineering roles among the fastest-growing salary brackets.
stories.blogAngle: Engineering Salaries Are Climbing in 2026 — Where Gas and Heating Engineers Stand
stories.notes: NEW 2026-05-20. Broader engineering focus but supports the skills-shortage-drives-pay narrative. Pair with story #11 for gas-specific salary data.

stories.title: Boiler Upgrade Scheme Overhauled — Major Changes from April 2026
stories.summary: Comprehensive overview of all BUS changes effective April 2026. Grant amounts, eligibility criteria, process changes, and what MCS installers need to know. Consolidates multiple BUS amendment announcements into a single reference.
stories.blogAngle: The Boiler Upgrade Scheme Just Changed — Here's What Installers Need to Know
stories.notes: NEW 2026-05-20. Complements story #4 (original BUS piece, now drafted as News 5). Could update/supersede that draft or run as standalone.

stories.title: BUS 2026 Changes — What Heating Companies Need to Know
stories.summary: Practical breakdown of April 2026 BUS amendments from an installer perspective. Covers new grant amounts, changed eligibility rules, and operational impacts for heating companies applying on behalf of homeowners.
stories.blogAngle: BUS Grant Changes in April 2026 — A Practical Guide for Heating Installers
stories.notes: NEW 2026-05-20. Installer-focused angle (not homeowner). Good complement to story #39.

stories.title: UK Decouples Gas From Electricity Pricing — Major Energy Market Reform
stories.summary: UK government announces decoupling of gas and electricity wholesale prices. Expected to reduce electricity costs relative to gas, making heat pumps more cost-competitive vs gas boilers. Major structural change to the energy market with direct implications for the gas-to-heat-pump transition.
stories.blogAngle: Gas and Electricity Prices Are Being Decoupled — What This Means for Heating Engineers
stories.notes: NEW 2026-05-20. SIGNIFICANT policy shift. Makes heat pump training even more urgent as economics shift. Updates context for story #26 (BBC heat pump cost analysis).

stories.title: HVAC Skills Shortages Persist Across UK and Global Markets
stories.summary: Global HVAC skills shortage analysis with UK-specific data. Demand for qualified heating, ventilation and air conditioning engineers continues to outstrip supply. Ageing workforce, net-zero transition, and construction boom all contributing factors. UK particularly affected in heat pump and refrigeration sectors.
stories.blogAngle: The HVAC Skills Crisis Isn't Just a UK Problem — But It Hits Hardest Here
stories.notes: NEW 2026-05-20. SKILLS SHORTAGE FOCUS. Adds global context to the UK shortage narrative. Complements stories #29, #30, #31.

stories.title: Growth and Skills Levy Replaces Apprenticeship Levy — April 2026 Reforms
stories.summary: Growth and Skills Levy reforms from April 2026 replace the Apprenticeship Levy. Broader funding for shorter courses and modular training, not just full apprenticeships. Relevant for employers funding gas, plumbing and heat pump training for existing staff.
stories.blogAngle: The Apprenticeship Levy Is Gone — How the New Growth and Skills Levy Funds Trade Training
stories.notes: NEW 2026-05-20. Relevant for employers considering funding staff training at BTS. Complements stories #25 and #28.

stories.title: BUS Amendments 2026 — Logic4training Analysis
stories.summary: Logic4training's analysis of the 2026 Boiler Upgrade Scheme amendments. Covers changes to grant amounts, eligibility, and process. Competitor content — their angle focuses on their own training courses.
stories.blogAngle: BUS Grant Amendments 2026 — What's Changed and What It Means for Your Heat Pump Training
stories.notes: NEW 2026-05-20. COMPETITOR CONTENT (Logic4training). Use for competitive intelligence — do NOT link to this source. Our angle must differentiate.

stories.title: Boiler Upgrade Scheme Budget Increase — More Grants Available from April 2026
stories.summary: UK Government increased the BUS budget from April 2026. More grant funding available for heat pump installations. Signals continued government commitment to heat pump rollout and need for qualified installers.
stories.blogAngle: More Heat Pump Grants Than Ever — Government Increases BUS Budget for 2026/27
stories.notes: NEW 2026-05-20. Official GOV.UK source. Updates context for stories #4, #38, #39. Installer demand angle — more grants = more installations = need more trained engineers.

stories.title: £50M Government Emergency Fund for Heating Oil Crisis — April 2026
stories.summary: £50M emergency support from 1 April 2026 for households struggling with heating oil/LPG costs. Scottish Emergency Oil Heating Scheme also launches 1 April. Heating oil still ~129-140p/litre (was 55-65p in Feb). 1.7M off-grid homes affected. Drives case for alternative heating systems and qualified installers.
stories.blogAngle: Government Announces £50M Heating Oil Emergency Fund — What This Means for the Heating Industry
stories.notes: Very current — 1 April 2026. Extends story #20. Good for showing industry disruption driving demand for trained heating engineers.

stories.title: Apprenticeship Funding Changes from April 2026 — New Starters Affected
stories.summary: Several updates to apprenticeship funding and contributions for new starters from 1 April 2026.
stories.blogAngle: Apprenticeship Funding Is Changing — What April 2026 Means for New Starters
stories.notes: Schema fixed 2026-05-20 (date→datePublished, category normalised, missing fields added). Drafted as pending News 6: content/pending-drafts/news-6-apprenticeship-funding-april-2026.md.

stories.title: 72% of UK Construction SMEs Hit by Skills Shortages — 49% Reporting Job Delays
stories.summary: 72% of SME construction firms now affected by skills shortages (up from 61% in H1 2025). 49% of builders report job delays. 22% have cancelled jobs entirely due to lack of skilled workers. Plumbers/HVAC listed as 3rd hardest trade to recruit (23% of firms struggling). Over 140,000 vacancies across the sector.
stories.blogAngle: Nearly Half of UK Builders Are Delaying Jobs — Here's Why Trained Engineers Are in Such High Demand
stories.notes: Very recent (31 Mar). Strong data-driven piece. Plumbers/HVAC specifically called out. Perfect for career changers — massive demand, not enough supply.

stories.title: Future Homes Standard Published — Heat Pumps Mandatory in New Builds from March 2028
stories.summary: UK Government published the Future Homes Standard. From March 2028, most new homes must have heat pumps or heat networks — gas boilers effectively ruled out. Solar PV mandatory. 75-80% carbon reduction. Industry widely supportive but flags skills shortage. MCS reports 100,000+ certified new-build installations last year. NAPIT says 'significant growth strands' for installers.
stories.blogAngle: The Future Homes Standard Is Here — What It Means for Gas and Heating Engineers
stories.notes: Published 27 March 2026. This replaces story #3 as the primary FHS piece. Drafted as insight-1.

stories.title: Apprenticeship Funding Reforms March 2026 — New Incentives and Rules
stories.summary: New government apprenticeship reforms aim to increase opportunities, address skills shortages, and better target funding. Changes to employer incentives and training requirements. Relevant for MLP route and new entrants.
stories.blogAngle: Apprenticeship Rules Just Changed — What It Means for Gas and Plumbing Training in 2026
stories.notes: Very recent (25 Mar). Links to MLP course. Good for career changers and employers considering training staff.

stories.title: BBC: Heat Pumps Work But They're Not Yet a Money Saver
stories.summary: BBC analysis — heat pumps work well technically but running costs aren't yet cheaper than gas for most homes. Heat pumps best at 45°C vs gas boilers at 70°C. Depends on tariff, insulation, and system design. Balanced view from real users.
stories.blogAngle: Are Heat Pumps Actually Cheaper Than Gas? What the BBC Found — And What Installers Should Know
targetKeywords: heat pump vs gas boiler cost 2026
stories.notes: BBC = high authority source. Balanced angle avoids pushing heat pumps unrealistically. Good for showing BTS gives honest industry advice.

stories.title: Top 5 MCS Umbrella Schemes for UK Heat Pump Installers (2026)
stories.summary: Roundup of the 5 main MCS umbrella schemes — including HPIN, Cinergi and others. Explains how umbrella schemes work: no need for individual MCS cert, but you still need training, insurance (£2M+), and Gas Safe/F-Gas registration. Shows the accessible routes into heat pump work.
stories.blogAngle: MCS Umbrella Schemes Explained — How to Start Installing Heat Pumps Without Full Certification
stories.notes: Practical piece for gas engineers considering adding heat pumps. Still need training even with umbrella.

stories.title: Heating Oil & LPG Crisis — Prices Triple as 1.7M Off-Grid Homes Hit Hard
stories.summary: Heating oil prices nearly tripled in a single week — from 55-65p/litre in February to 155p/litre by 9 March. 1.7M UK households without mains gas affected. Martin Lewis presenting dossier to government. LPG also spiking. Drives home the case for alternative heating systems.
stories.blogAngle: Heating Oil Prices Triple — Why Off-Grid Homes Are Looking at Heat Pumps and LPG Alternatives
stories.notes: Very current — March 2026. Strong hook for LPG changeover course. Links heat pump training as alternative for off-grid homes.

stories.title: MCS Redeveloped Installer Scheme — Phased Rollout 2026-2027
stories.summary: MCS is overhauling its installer certification scheme. New 'Redeveloped Installer Scheme' being rolled out in phases through 2026 and into 2027. IAA (Installation Assurance Authority) is UKAS-accredited certification body. Changes to how installers get and maintain MCS certification.
stories.blogAngle: MCS Certification Is Changing — What Heat Pump Installers Need to Know in 2026
stories.notes: Technical but important for anyone considering heat pump certification. Links to heat pump course.

stories.title: Gas Engineer Skills Shortage Persists — Construction Industry Crisis
stories.summary: Skills shortages remain one of the biggest issues facing construction. Qualified gas engineer shortfall is a significant contributor. Aging workforce, growing demand, energy transition all factors. Career still worth it in 2026.
stories.blogAngle: Is a Career in Gas Engineering Still Worth It in 2026? (Spoiler: Yes)
stories.notes: NOTE: Able Skills (competitor #4) published this already. Our angle needs to be unique — focus on multi-trade + heat pump upskilling, not just gas alone.

stories.title: Half of UK Gas Engineers Over 55 — Mass Retirement Crisis Looming
stories.sourceLabel: Gas Training & Assessment / Gas Safe Register
stories.summary: Gas Safe Register data shows half of all registered gas engineers are over 55. Within 10 years the majority will have retired. Fewer young people entering the trade. Lower birth rates compounding the problem. Creates massive replacement demand on top of growth demand from net-zero transition.
stories.blogAngle: Half of Gas Engineers Will Retire Within 10 Years — Who's Going to Replace Them?
stories.notes: SKILLS SHORTAGE FOCUS. Powerful stat — half the workforce retiring. Perfect for career changers and MLP students. Shows urgency.

stories.title: CITB Construction Skills Gap Report — Feb 2026 Update
stories.summary: CITB's latest construction skills gap report confirms ongoing workforce shortfalls across trades. Gas, plumbing and HVAC among the hardest-hit sectors. Report calls for increased training capacity and employer engagement. Data supports career-change messaging.
stories.blogAngle: The CITB's Latest Skills Report Confirms What We Already Know — The Industry Needs More Engineers
stories.notes: NEW 2026-05-20. Adds to the CITB data from story #31. Fix Radio is a trade-focused source — good credibility.

stories.title: Ofgem Energy Price Cap Falls 7% from April 2026 — Bills Down £117
stories.summary: Ofgem price cap drops to £1,641/year from 1 April 2026, down 7% (£117). First significant fall in years. Gas at 5.9p/kWh, electricity at 27.7p/kWh. July could rise again. Context for heat pump running cost comparisons.
stories.blogAngle: Energy Bills Are Falling — But Is Gas Still Cheaper Than a Heat Pump in 2026?
targetKeywords: gas vs heat pump running costs
stories.notes: Good context piece. Logic4training already covered 'Is Gas or Electric Cheaper in 2026' — our angle needs to be installer-focused, not homeowner.

stories.title: Gas Engineer Salaries 2026 — Average £42,854, Self-Employed £58.57/hr
stories.summary: Average gas engineer salary £42,854/year (Indeed, Jan 2026). Self-employed average £58.57/hr. Apprentices £16,500-£21,000. Heat pump certified earn £7-10K more. Skills shortage driving competitive pay.
stories.blogAngle: What Do Gas Engineers Really Earn in 2026? The Numbers Behind the Trade
stories.notes: High search volume career research keyword. Perfect for career changers considering MLP.

stories.title: Higher Labour Costs Forecast as Skills Shortage Meets Rising Construction Demand
stories.summary: Rising construction demand colliding with deepening skills shortage is pushing labour costs up. Wages rising across trades as firms compete for qualified workers. Gas engineers and HVAC specialists among the most in-demand. Salary growth of ~4% expected over 2025 levels.
stories.blogAngle: Trades Wages Are Rising Fast — What the Skills Shortage Means for Your Earning Power
stories.notes: SKILLS SHORTAGE FOCUS. Money angle — shortage = higher pay. Great for career changers weighing up training investment vs salary return.

stories.title: UK Needs 33,700 Full-Time Heat Pump Installers Now — Has Around 2,000
stories.summary: UK has estimated 4,000-10,000 trained heat pump installers, but only ~2,000 working full-time. Needs 33,700 full-time installers short-term, 70,000 by 2035. Government target of 600,000 installations per year by 2028 impossible without massive workforce expansion. Retraining existing Gas Safe engineers (130,000 registered) is the fastest path.
stories.blogAngle: 2,000 Full-Time Heat Pump Installers vs 33,700 Needed — The UK's Most Extreme Skills Gap
stories.notes: SKILLS SHORTAGE FOCUS. The 2,000 vs 33,700 stat is incredibly powerful. Perfect for gas engineers considering upskilling. Links directly to heat pump course.

stories.title: 2026 Heating Landscape for Installers — Vokèra Industry Analysis
stories.summary: Vokèra marketing director Neil Mattock analyses the 2026 installer landscape. Gas boiler ban headlines created uncertainty but reality is more nuanced — both challenges and opportunities. Transition period means multi-skilled engineers are best positioned.
stories.blogAngle: The 2026 Heating Landscape: Challenges, Opportunities and Where to Focus Your Training
stories.notes: Good thought-leadership angle. Manufacturer perspective adds authority.

stories.title: 750,000 Construction Workers to Retire by 2036 — Industry Needs 48,000 New Workers Per Year
stories.summary: 750,000 construction workers expected to retire by 2036. Sector needs ~48,000 new workers annually. Under-19% of workers are under 25, while 35% are over 50. Employment has declined 10.8% since the pandemic. Young people not entering the industry. Government responding with Growth and Skills Levy and 30+ Homebuilding Skills Hubs.
stories.blogAngle: 750,000 Tradespeople Retiring in 10 Years — Why Construction Is Crying Out for New Blood
stories.notes: SKILLS SHORTAGE FOCUS. Broader construction but directly relevant. Career changer angle — massive opportunity for new entrants. Links to MLP.

stories.title: Worcester Bosch Compress Series 2026 — Heat Pump Range Review
stories.summary: Worcester Bosch 2026 Compress series air-to-water heat pumps. Vaillant aroTHERM models from £3,500, SCOP up to 5.2, A+++ efficiency. Major manufacturers investing heavily in heat pump ranges.
stories.blogAngle: Vaillant, Worcester Bosch, Mitsubishi — Why Boiler Brands Are Going All-In on Heat Pumps
stories.notes: Marked stale 2026-05-25 (product launch >3 months old, weak training angle, superseded by newer product launches e.g. Viessmann ASHP at InstallerSHOW June 2026).

stories.title: Plumbing Apprenticeship Starts Crashed 37% — Industry Sounds Alarm
stories.summary: Plumbing apprenticeship starts dropped 37% since 2021/22. Government figures show only a few hundred completions this academic year. APHC warns of worsening workforce pipeline at a time when demand is rising for housing, water and net-zero work. National Apprenticeship Week 2026 context.
stories.blogAngle: Plumbing Apprenticeships Down 37% — What This Means for the Trade and Your Career
stories.notes: Strong career-change angle. Links directly to MLP course. Shows demand outstripping supply = opportunity. Drafted 12 Apr 2026 and published as News 4 on 11 May 2026.

stories.title: Warm Homes: Local Grant — Up to £15,000 for Low-Income Households
stories.summary: Warm Homes Local Grant provides up to £15,000 for insulation and heat pumps. Income under £36,000, EPC D-G required. Funding allocated to local authorities to deliver. Includes air source heat pump installation.
stories.blogAngle: Local Authorities Are Funding Heat Pump Installations — Is Your Training Ready?
stories.notes: Same grant caution. Installer demand angle, not homeowner eligibility.

stories.title: CITB: UK Needs 59,000 Extra HVAC & Plumbing Professionals
stories.summary: Construction Industry Training Board (CITB) data: 59,000 additional HVAC technicians and plumbing professionals needed across the UK within the next few years. Green Building Boom driving demand — net-zero retrofit, new builds, heat pump installations. Skills gap widening as demand outstrips training capacity.
stories.blogAngle: 59,000 More Plumbers and HVAC Engineers Needed — The Numbers Behind the UK's Biggest Skills Gap
stories.notes: SKILLS SHORTAGE FOCUS. CITB = authoritative source. 59,000 is a concrete number that makes strong headlines. Links to MLP and heat pump courses.

stories.title: Boiler Upgrade Scheme — £7,500 for Heat Pumps, New £2,500 for Air-to-Air
stories.summary: BUS offers £7,500 for air-to-water heat pumps, £7,500 for ground source. New £2,500 grant for air-to-air systems in 2026. EPC requirement removed. Homeowners need MCS-certified installers.
stories.blogAngle: £7,500 Heat Pump Grants — Are You Qualified to Install Them?
stories.notes: NOTE: Sunny's rule = no government funding claims on BTS content. Angle must be about installer qualifications, not homeowner grants. Drafted as pending News 5: content/pending-drafts/news-5-bus-amendments-2026.md.

stories.title: ECO4 Scheme Extended to 31 December 2026
stories.summary: ECO4 extended 9 months to 31 Dec 2026. Free insulation, heating, solar for eligible low-income homes. No confirmed successor scheme. Drives demand for qualified installers.
stories.blogAngle: ECO4 Extended — More Demand for Qualified Heating Installers Than Ever
stories.notes: Same grant caution as #4. Focus on installer demand, not homeowner eligibility.

stories.title: Warm Homes Plan — £15bn Government Investment in Home Energy Upgrades
stories.summary: UK Government published the Warm Homes Plan — £15bn over 5 years to upgrade 5 million homes. Includes heat pumps, insulation, solar panels, batteries. Biggest home upgrade programme in British history.
stories.blogAngle: What the Warm Homes Plan Means for Gas Engineers and Heating Installers
stories.notes: Major story — high search volume. Links to heat pump training and multi-trade upskilling.

stories.title: Heat Pump Training Drop-Off: 39% of Qualified Engineers Don't Install After Training
stories.summary: Nesta research: over 9,000 people completed heat pump training in 2024 (up 15% from 2023), but 39% drop-off rate — nearly 4 in 10 don't go on to install. 61% of installers say admin/compliance tasks reduce capacity. 59% report shortage of skilled staff. 130,000 Gas Safe engineers could retrain — the fastest route to expanding national installation capacity.
stories.blogAngle: Thousands Are Training in Heat Pumps — But Nearly 40% Never Install. Here's What's Going Wrong
stories.notes: SKILLS SHORTAGE FOCUS. Interesting angle — training alone isn't enough. BTS can position around practical, job-ready training. Nesta is authoritative source.

stories.title: Part L Building Regulations & Future Homes Standard — Legislation December 2026
stories.summary: Future Homes Standard published autumn 2025. Legislation laid December 2025, comes into effect December 2026 with December 2027 transition end. 75-85% carbon reduction vs 2013. No gas boilers in new builds. All extensions and loft conversions must meet new energy standards too.
stories.blogAngle: Part L Is Changing — What Heating Engineers Need to Know Before December 2026
stories.notes: Marked stale 2026-05-25 (superseded by story #19 — actual FHS publication Mar 2026). Pre-publication speculation no longer needed.

stories.title: Future Homes Standard — New Builds Can't Have Gas Boilers from 2027
stories.summary: Future Homes Standard mandates all new homes built to low-carbon heating standards. Gas boilers phased out for new builds. Legislation expected December 2026, transition ends December 2027. 75-85% carbon cut vs 2013 levels.
stories.blogAngle: Future Homes Standard Explained — What It Means for Heating Engineers
stories.notes: Marked stale 2026-05-25 (superseded by story #19 — actual FHS publication Mar 2026). Pre-publication story from Dec 2025 no longer needed.

stories.title: HPIN Partners with City Plumbing — Heat Pump Access for Boiler Installers
stories.summary: HPIN and City Plumbing strategic partnership. Gives thousands of boiler installers access to heat pump market without joining MCS or paying umbrella fees.
stories.blogAngle: City Plumbing + HPIN — What This Partnership Means for Boiler Engineers
stories.notes: Marked stale 2026-05-20 (>5 months old, borderline). Niche but relevant — shows industry momentum. Pair with #7 or use as standalone.

stories.title: MCS Certification Fund — 75% of Cert Fees Covered (Scotland, Expanding)
stories.summary: Scottish Government MCS Certification Fund pays 75% (up to £1,000) of heat pump certification fees. Runs until March 2026. Addresses barrier of certification cost for smaller firms.
stories.blogAngle: MCS Certification Made Cheaper — How Grants Are Lowering the Barrier to Heat Pump Work
stories.notes: Marked stale 2026-05-20. Scotland-specific fund likely expired (ran until March 2026). May need verification before use.

stories.title: Worcester Bosch Launches VAULT — Enhanced Installer Loyalty Scheme
stories.summary: Worcester Bosch evolved its loyalty programme to VAULT. More rewards and support for installers. Shows manufacturers investing in installer relationships as heat pump transition accelerates.
stories.blogAngle: Manufacturer Loyalty Schemes for Installers — Are They Worth Joining?
stories.notes: Marked stale 2026-05-20 (>7 months old). Lower priority — niche topic.

stories.title: Heat Pump Installer Shortage — Only 3,000 Qualified vs 600,000 Target
stories.summary: Only ~3,000 MCS-certified heat pump installers in UK. Government target is 600,000 installations per year by 2028. Certified installers earn £7,000-£10,000 more annually. Massive skills gap = opportunity.
stories.blogAngle: 3,000 Heat Pump Installers vs 600,000 Target — The UK's Biggest Skills Gap
stories.notes: Marked stale 2026-05-20 (>8 months old). Data superseded by story #34 (Nesta, Feb 2026) with more recent figures.

stories.title: EDF Launches HPIN Direct — MCS Umbrella Scheme for Smaller Installers
stories.summary: EDF Heat Pumps launched HPIN Direct — umbrella scheme letting smaller firms install heat pumps without individual MCS certification. EDF handles design, commissioning, MCS cert and BUS applications. No subscription fees.
stories.blogAngle: HPIN Direct — How Smaller Heating Firms Can Now Access Heat Pump Work
stories.notes: Marked stale 2026-05-20 (>8 months old). Still relevant context but scheme details may have changed. Pair with story #27 (MCS Umbrella overview, Mar 2026).

stories.title: Clean Heat Market Mechanism (CHMM) — Launched April 2025
stories.summary: CHMM requires boiler manufacturers to sell 6% heat pumps relative to boiler sales in 2025/2026. Dubbed 'boiler tax' but actually targets manufacturers, not homeowners. Drives manufacturer investment in heat pump training and installer networks.
stories.blogAngle: The 'Boiler Tax' Explained — What the Clean Heat Market Mechanism Really Means
stories.notes: Marked stale 2026-05-20 (>13 months old). CHMM may have been updated since April 2025. Verify current status before use.

stories.title: Gas Boiler Ban Scrapped — No 2035 Phase-Out for Existing Homes
stories.summary: Labour government scrapped the 2035 gas boiler ban. No forced removal of working boilers. Replaced with Warm Homes Plan and incentive-based approach. Gas engineers still needed for decades.
stories.blogAngle: The Gas Boiler Ban Is Dead — What Does This Actually Mean for Your Career?
stories.notes: Marked stale 2026-05-20 (>16 months old). Evergreen topic — people still searching for clarity. Data may need refreshing but the core story hasn't changed.

stories.title: F-Gas Regulation Changes — HFC Refrigerant Bans Tightening
stories.summary: From Jan 2025, single-split AC systems with GWP >750 refrigerants banned. UK aligning with EU Regulation 2024/573. Affects heat pump and AC installers. More changes expected 2026.
stories.blogAngle: F-Gas Changes in 2025/26 — What Heat Pump and AC Installers Need to Know
stories.notes: Marked stale 2026-05-20 (>16 months old). Technical audience. Good for positioning BTS as industry-aware. Logic4training already covers this. Verify if 2026 changes have landed.

stories.title: Gas Safety Regulations (GSIUR) — 2024 Amendment Updates
stories.summary: GSIUR amendments include updated Wobbe Number limits (delayed to April 2025), expanded training scope to cover water/electricity/asbestos dangers, and new 5-yearly review duty for Secretary of State.
stories.blogAngle: Gas Safety Regulations Updated — What's Changed and Why It Matters for Your ACS
stories.notes: Marked stale 2026-05-20 (>25 months old). Regulations may have been further updated since April 2024. Verify with HSE before use.
